cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A roadside inn built at day-march intervals along Silk Road routes where merchants, their pack animals, and goods could rest safely overnight, fostering long-distance trade across Central Asia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A group of merchants traveling together with pack animals suited to arid terrain, enabling the movement of luxury goods across the deserts and oases of the Silk Roads

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Raw materials or basic traded goods exchanged in bulk across Afro-Eurasian networks, as distinct from finished luxury products

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Chinese chemical innovation first developed under the Tang and Song that diffused westward along Silk Road routes, transforming siege warfare across Eurasia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A hard, white, translucent ceramic produced in China that became one of the most prized Chinese exports along the Silk Roads and Indian Ocean routes

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Chinese technology for manufacturing writing material from plant fibers that diffused to the Islamic world after the Battle of Talas and eventually to Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Chinese navigational instrument using a magnetized needle that diffused westward through Silk Road and Indian Ocean networks, revolutionizing long-distance maritime travel

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A 751 CE clash between Abbasid and Tang forces in Central Asia whose most lasting consequence was the transfer of Chinese papermaking technology to the Islamic world

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Financial institutions that emerged in Song China and the Islamic world to issue paper money, letters of credit, and loans that lubricated long-distance Silk Road commerce

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The tiny insects whose cocoons supplied the raw thread for China's most prized export and whose cultivation was a closely guarded imperial secret for centuries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A major oasis city in the Tarim Basin at the junction of northern and southern Silk Road branches, serving as a key market linking China with Central Asia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A wealthy Central Asian Silk Road hub famous for paper, textiles, and mosques; later became the capital of Tamerlane's empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A devastating 14th-century pandemic that spread from Inner Asia along Silk Road and maritime routes, killing roughly one-third of Europe's population and disrupting Eurasian trade networks

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An Iranian-speaking merchant people of Central Asia who dominated overland Silk Road commerce for centuries, spreading languages, scripts, and religions along their routes

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A late-13th-century Venetian travelogue describing the author's years at the Yuan court and the wonders of Mongol-ruled China, widely read in Europe and later influencing Atlantic explorers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 14th-century Moroccan Muslim scholar whose decades of travel across the Dar al-Islam — from West Africa to China — produced one of the most important travel accounts of the medieval world

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The late-13th-century Venetian merchant whose reported travels to the court of Kublai Khan introduced European readers to the cities, customs, and wealth of Mongol-era Asia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A small lateen-rigged Arab sailing vessel with a sewn wooden hull that carried goods and people across the Indian Ocean using the seasonal monsoon winds

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A navigational instrument refined by Muslim astronomers that allowed mariners to determine latitude by measuring the altitude of the sun or stars

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A triangular sail, first used on Arab dhows, that allowed ships to sail effectively against the wind and became critical to Indian Ocean and Mediterranean shipping

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Chinese innovation mounted at the rear of a ship that replaced the steering oar, giving captains far greater control over larger ocean-going vessels

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The predictable seasonal wind patterns that reversed direction between summer and winter, enabling merchants to plan round-trip voyages across the Indian Ocean basin

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A large, flat-bottomed Chinese ocean-going ship with multiple masts and watertight compartments, used by Song, Yuan, and Ming mariners across East Asian and Indian Ocean waters

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The dispersion of a people beyond their ancestral homeland, illustrated in this period by Arab, Chinese, and Indian merchant communities settling in Indian Ocean port cities

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ming admiral and eunuch who led seven massive state-sponsored treasure voyages across the Indian Ocean in the early 15th century, projecting Chinese power as far as East Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A way of life based on herding domesticated animals and seasonal migration across grasslands, characteristic of Inner Asian peoples such as the Mongols

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The vast belt of semi-arid grassland stretching across Central Asia that served as the homeland and highway of nomadic peoples including the Mongols

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A traditional Mongol assembly of tribal leaders that elected new great khans and ratified major political decisions

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Any of the four major political divisions the Mongol Empire split into among Genghis Khan's descendants, each ruled by a senior member of the imperial family

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Mongol khanate established over Russia and the Pontic steppe that collected tribute from Russian princes for more than two centuries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The military technique in which armies surrounded fortified cities and cut off their supplies, refined by the Mongols through catapults, trebuchets, and gunpowder weapons borrowed from conquered peoples

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Mongol-ruled Chinese dynasty founded by Kublai Khan that governed China from 1271 to 1368 and integrated it into the broader Mongol world

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Mongol khanate centered in Persia and Mesopotamia that ruled the former Abbasid lands and eventually converted to Islam

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Mongol network of mounted relay stations spaced a day's ride apart that carried messages, officials, and merchants quickly across the empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A metal passport issued to Mongol envoys and favored merchants guaranteeing safe passage, food, and lodging throughout the empire's relay network

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The secret code of Mongol law attributed to Genghis Khan that governed conduct, succession, and military discipline across the empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The period of relative stability across Mongol-ruled Eurasia in the 13th and 14th centuries during which long-distance trade, travel, and cultural exchange flourished

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The early-13th-century ruler who unified the Mongol tribes, reorganized them into a decimal military system, and launched the conquests that built the largest contiguous land empire in history

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Genghis Khan's most celebrated general, whose long-range campaigns swept through Khwarazm, the Caucasus, Russia, and Eastern Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The early-13th-century ruler of a wealthy Central Asian empire whose execution of Mongol envoys provoked a devastating invasion by Genghis Khan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The grandson of Genghis Khan who completed the conquest of Song China, founded the Yuan Dynasty, and hosted Marco Polo at his court in Khanbaliq

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The late-14th-century Turko-Mongol conqueror based in Samarkand whose brutal campaigns devastated much of Central Asia, Persia, and northern India
